reasoning: The base rate for a US military invasion of a large sovereign nation like Venezuela is extremely low. Such an operation would require months of visible troop buildups, complex logistics, and extensive political debate or congressional authorization. Under previous US administrations, even during periods of maximum pressure on the Maduro regime, military intervention was never carried out. Given the knowledge cutoff is October 26, 2025, there are only two months remaining in the year. If an invasion were imminent, large-scale mobilizations and explicit warnings would have already occurred. Even then, the appetite for large-scale military conflict in Latin America is practically nonexistent. Thus, the probability of the US initiating a military offensive to control Venezuelan territory by December 31, 2025, is close to zero.
